questions.explanation: Your mother's mother (or your father's mother) is your grandmother; her husband is your grandfather. An aunt is your parent's sister, a niece is your sibling's daughter, and a cousin is your aunt's or uncle's child — all one generation closer than a grandparent.

questions.prompt: Find the intruder (the word that is NOT a member of the family).
questions.explanation: Daughter, son and cousin are all members of a family. Doctor is a job, so it is the intruder. The trap is that doctor sounds a little like daughter, but they belong to different fields — family versus jobs.

questions.prompt: Complete the common phrase: "a ___ of milk".
questions.explanation: Milk is a drink you pour, so we say a glass of milk. A slice, a loaf and a piece go with solid food like bread — you can never say "a piece of milk". Match the measure word to whether the thing is a drink or a solid.

questions.prompt: Which word is a synonym of "difficult"?
questions.explanation: Hard is a synonym of difficult (not easy to do): a hard quest is a difficult quest. Easy is its opposite, fast is the opposite of slow, and cheap is the opposite of expensive — so only hard means the same as difficult.

questions.prompt: Put these in counting order. Which number is the ordinal "third"?
questions.explanation: The ordinal third refers to position number three: first (1), second (2), third (3). One and two are the cardinal numbers before it, and "free" is a different word (it means costing no money), not a number — a common spelling and sound trap.

questions.prompt: Find the intruder (the word that is NOT a drink).
questions.explanation: Water, coffee and tea are all drinks. Rice is a food you eat, so it is the intruder. The trap is that all four are things you have at meals, but only rice is eaten, not drunk.
